Name: Jenkins < 2.154 and < 2.138.4 LTS Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
description: Jenkins is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.   Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%

impact: Aected Software/OS

Name: Jenkins < 2.243, < 2.235.5 LTS Buer Corruption in bundled Jetty - Linux
description: Jenkins is prone to a buer corruption in bundled Jetty.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Jenkins < 2.243, < 2.235.5 LTS Buffer Corruption in bundled Jetty - Linux
impact: This vulnerability may allow unauthenticated attackers to obtain HTTP response headers that may include sensitive data intended for another user.
solution: Update to version 2.243, 2.235.5 LTS or later.
insight: In Eclipse Jetty in case of too large response headers, Jetty throws an exception to produce an HTTP 431 error. When this happens, the ByteBuer containing the HTTP response headers is released back to the ByteBuerPool twice. Because of this double release, two threads can acquire the same ByteBuer from the pool and while thread1 is about to use the ByteBuer to write response1 data, thread2 lls the ByteBuer with response2 data. Thread1 then proceeds to write the buer that now contains response2 data.
This results in client1, which issued request1 and expects responses, to see response2 which could contain sensitive data belonging to client2 (HTTP session ids, authentication credentials, etc.).

Name: Jenkins < 2.214, < 2.204.2 LTS Authentication Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
description: Jenkins is prone to an inbound TCP Agent Protocol/3 authentication bypass vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Jenkins < 2.214, < 2.204.2 LTS Authentication Bypass Vulnerability - Linux
solution: Update to version 2.214, 2.204.2 LTS or later.
insight: Jenkins includes support for the Inbound TCP Agent Protocol/3 for communication between master and agents. While this protocol has been deprecated in 2018 and was recently removed from Jenkins in 2.214, it could still easily be enabled in Jenkins LTS 2.204.1, 2.213, and older.

This protocol incorrectly reuses encryption parameters which allow an unauthenticated remote attacker to determine the connection secret. This secret can then be used to connect attacker-controlled Jenkins agents to the Jenkins master.

Name: Eclipse Jetty DoS Vulnerability (MadeYouReset) - Linux
description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability in the HTTP/2 protocol dubbed 'MadeYouReset'.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Eclipse Jetty DoS Vulnerability (MadeYouReset) - Linux
product_detection_result: Detected by Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP) (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1. ->0.800953)
insight: The vulnerability takes advantage of a design aw in the HTTP/2 protocol - While HTTP/2 has a limit on the number of concurrently active streams per connection (which is usually 100, and is set by the parameter SETTINGS_MAX_CONCURRENT_STREAMS), the number of active streams is not counted correctly - when a stream is reset, it is immediately considered not active, and thus unaccounted for in the active streams counter. While the protocol does not count those streams as active, the server's backend logic still processes and handles the requests that were canceled. Thus, the attacker can exploit this vulnerability to cause the server to handle an unbounded number of concurrent streams from a client on the same connection. The exploitation is very simple: the client issues a request in a stream, and then sends the control frame that causes the server to send a RST_STREAM.

Name: Jenkins < 2.528.3, 2.541 Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
description: Jenkins is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
solution: Update to version Jenkins 2.541, LTS 2.528.3 or later.
insight: The following aws exist:
- CVE-2025-67635: Jenkins does not properly close HTTP-based CLI connections when the connection stream becomes corrupted, allowing unauthenticated attackers to cause a denial of service.
- CVE-2025-67636: A missing permission check in Jenkins allows attackers with View/Read permission to view encrypted password values in views.

- CVE-2025-67637: Jenkins stores build authorization tokens unencrypted in job cong.xml les on the Jenkins controller where they can be viewed by users with Item/Extended Read permission or access to the Jenkins controller le system.
- CVE-2025-67638: Jenkins does not mask build authorization tokens displayed on the job con- guration form, increasing the potential for attackers to observe and capture them.
- CVE-2025-67639: A cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerability in Jenkins allows attackers to trick users into logging in to the attacker's account.

Name: Jenkins < 2.319.3, < 2.334 DoS Vulnerability - Linux
description: Jenkins is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
solution: Update to version 2.334, 2.319.3 LTS or later.
insight: Jenkins is aected by the XStream library's vulnerability CVE-2021-43859. This library is used by Jenkins to serialize and deserialize various XML les, like global and job cong.xml, build.xml, and numerous others.
This allows attackers able to submit crafted XML les to Jenkins to be parsed as conguration, e.g. through the POST cong.xml API, to cause a denial of service (DoS).

Name: Eclipse Jetty DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-26vr-8j45-3r4w) - Linux

Name: Eclipse Jetty HTTP/2 Protocol DoS Vulnerability (CVE-2023-44487) - Linux
description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability in the HTTP/2 protocol.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Eclipse Jetty HTTP/2 Protocol DoS Vulnerability (CVE-2023-44487) - Linux
product_detection_result: Method: Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP)
impact: This vulnerability allows a remote, unauthenticated attacker to cause an increase in CPU usage that can lead to a denial-of-service (DoS).
insight: The HTTP/2 protocol allows a denial of service (server resource consumption) because request cancellation can reset many streams quickly, as exploited in the wild in August through October 2023.
The aw is also known as HTTP/2 Rapid Reset Attack.

Name: Eclipse Jetty DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-rggv-cv7r-mw98) - Linux
description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to a denial of service (DoS) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Eclipse Jetty DoS Vulnerability (GHSA-rggv-cv7r-mw98) - Linux OID:1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.151836 Version used: 2025-02-10T05:38:01Z
product_detection_result: Detected by Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP) (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.800953)
impact: If an HTTP/2 connection gets TCP congested, when an idle timeout occurs the HTTP/2 session is marked as closed, and then a GOAWAY frame is queued to be written. However it is not written because the connection is TCP congested. When another idle timeout period elapses, it is then supposed to hard close the connection, but it delegates to the HTTP/2 session which reports that it has already been closed so it does not attempt to hard close the connection. This leaves the connection in ESTABLISHED state (i.e. not closed), TCP congested, and idle. An attacker can cause many connections to end up in this state, and the server may run out of le descriptors, eventually causing the server to stop accepting new connections from valid clients.
insight: If an HTTP/2 connection gets TCP congested, when an idle timeout occurs the HTTP/2 session is marked as closed, and then a GOAWAY frame is queued to be written. However it is not written because the connection is TCP congested. When another idle timeout period elapses, it is then supposed to hard close the connection, but it delegates to the HTTP/2 session which reports that it has already been closed so it does not attempt to hard close the connection. This leaves the connection in ESTABLISHED state (i.e. not closed), TCP congested, and idle. An attacker can cause many connections to end up in this state, and the server may run out of le descriptors, eventually causing the server to stop accepting new connections from valid clients.

Name: Jenkins HTTP/2 DoS Vulnerability (CVE-2022-2048) - Linux
description: Jenkins is prone to an HTTP/2 denial of service (DoS) vulnerability in Jetty.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Jenkins HTTP/2 DoS Vulnerability (CVE-2022-2048) - Linux
solution: Aected Software/OS Jenkins version 2.346.3 (LTS) and prior and 2.362 and prior.
insight: Jenkins bundles Winstone-Jetty, a wrapper around Jetty, to act as HTTP and servlet server when started using java -jar jenkins.war. This is how Jenkins is run when using any of the installers or packages, but not when run using servlet containers such as Tomcat. Jenkins bundle versions of Jetty aected by the security vulnerability CVE-2022-2048. This vulnerability allows unauthenticated attackers to make the Jenkins UI unresponsive by exploiting Jetty's handling of invalid HTTP/2 requests, causing a denial of service.

Name: Jenkins < 2.197 and < 2.176.4 LTS Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
description: Jenkins is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Jenkins < 2.197 and < 2.176.4 LTS Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
solution: Update Jenkins weekly to version 2.197 or later / Jenkins LTS to version 2.176.4 or later.
insight: Jenkins is prone to the following vulnerabilities:

- The f:expandableTextBox form control interpreted its content as HTML when expanded, resulting in a stored XSS vulnerability exploitable by users with permission to dene its contents, typically Job/Congure. (CVE-2019-10401)
- The f:combobox form control interpreted its item labels as HTML, resulting in a stored XSS vulnerability exploitable by users with permission to dene its contents. (CVE-2019-10402)

- The SCM tag name on the tooltip for SCM tag actions was not being escaped, resulting in a stored XSS vulnerability exploitable by users able to control SCM tag names for these actions. (CVE-2019-10403)

- The reason why a queue item is blocked in tooltips was not being escaped, resulting in a stored XSS vulnerability exploitable by users able to control parts of the reason a queue item is blocked, such as label expressions not matching any idle executors. (CVE-2019-10404)

- The value of the 'Cookie' HTTP request header on the /whoAmI/ URL was being printed, allowing attackers exploiting another XSS vulnerability to obtain the HTTP session cookie despite it being marked as HttpOnly. (CVE-2019-10405)

- The values set as Jenkins URL in the global conguration were not being restricted or l- tered, resulting in a stored XSS vulnerability exploitable by attackers with Overall/Administer permission. (CVE-2019-10406)

Name: Jenkins < 2.276, < 2.263.3 Arbitrary File Read Vulnerability
description: Jenkins is prone to an arbitrary le read vulnerability.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Jenkins < 2.276, < 2.263.3 Arbitrary File Read Vulnerability
impact: The vulnerability allows attackers with Job/Workspace permission and the ability to control workspace contents, e.g., with Job/Congure permission or the ability to change SCM contents, to create symbolic links that allow them to access les outside workspaces using the workspace browser.
solution: Update to version 2.276, 2.263.3 LTS or later.
insight: Due to a time-of-check to time-of-use (TOCTOU) race condition, the le browser for workspaces, archived artifacts, and $JENKINS_HOME/userContent/ follows symbolic links to locations outside the directory being browsed in Jenkins.

Name: Eclipse Jetty Information Disclosure Vulnerability (GHSA-gwcr-j4wh-j3cq)
description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to an information disclosure vulnerability in the ConcatServlet and WelcomeFilter servlet.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 30%
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Eclipse Jetty Information Disclosure Vulnerability (GHSA-gwcr-j4wh-j3cq) OID:1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.146099
product_detection_result: Detected by Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP) (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.800953)
insight: Requests to the ConcatServlet and WelcomeFilter are able to access protected resources within the WEB-INF directory. For example a request to the ConcatServlet with a URI of /concat?/%2557EB-INF/web.xml can retrieve the web.xml le. This can reveal sensitive information regarding the implementation of a web application.

This occurs because both ConcatServlet and WelcomeFilter decode the supplied path to verify it is not within the WEB-INF or META-INF directories. It then uses this decoded path to call RequestDispatcher which will also do decoding of the path. This double decoding allows paths with a doubly encoded WEB-INF to bypass this security check.

Name: Eclipse Jetty Multiple Vulnerabilities (GHSA-qw69-rqj8-6qw8, GHSA-p26g-97m4-6q7c) - Linux
description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Eclipse Jetty Multiple Vulnerabilities (GHSA-qw69-rqj8-6qw8, GHSA-p26g-97m4-6q7..
product_detection_result: Method: Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP)
impact: Aected Software/OS Eclipse Jetty version 9.4.50 and prior, version 10.0.x through 10.0.13 and 11.0.x through 11.0.13.
insight: The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2023-26048: OutOfMemoryError for large multipart without lename read via re- quest.getParameter()
- CVE-2023-26049: Cookie parsing of quoted values can exltrate values from other cookies

Name: Eclipse Jetty HTTP Header Vulnerability (GHSA-hmr7-m48g-48f6) - Linux
description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to an HTTP header vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Eclipse Jetty HTTP Header Vulnerability (GHSA-hmr7-m48g-48f6) - Linux
product_detection_result: Method: Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP)
impact: Aected Software/OS Eclipse Jetty version 9.0.0 through 9.4.51, 10.0.0 through 10.0.15, 11.0.0 through 11.0.15 and version 12.0.0.
insight: Jetty accepts the '+' character proceeding the content-length value in a HTTP/1 header eld. This is more permissive than allowed by the RFC and other servers routinely reject such requests with 400 responses. There is no known exploit scenario, but it is conceivable that request smuggling could result if jetty is used in combination with a server that does not close the connection after sending such a 400 response.

Name: Eclipse Jetty URI Parsing Vulnerability (GHSA-qh8g-58pp-2wxh) - Linux
description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to an URI parsing vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Eclipse Jetty URI Parsing Vulnerability (GHSA-qh8g-58pp-2wxh) - Linux
product_detection_result: Detected by Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP) (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.
Method: Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP)
impact: Aected Software/OS
Eclipse Jetty version 7.0.0 through 12.0.11.
insight: Eclipse Jetty includes a utility class, HttpURI, for URI/URL parsing.
The HttpURI class does insucient validation on the authority segment of a URI. However the
behaviour of HttpURI diers from the common browsers in how it handles a URI that would be
considered invalid if fully validated against the RRC. Specically HttpURI and the browser may
dier on the value of the host extracted from an invalid URI and thus a combination of Jetty
and a vulnerable browser may be vulnerable to a open redirect attack or to a SSRF attack if the
URI is used after passing validation checks.

Name: Jenkins < 2.287, < 2.277.2 LTS Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
description: Jenkins is prone to multiple vulnerabilities.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Jenkins < 2.287, < 2.277.2 LTS Multiple Vulnerabilities - Linux
impact: Aected Software/OS
Jenkins prior to version 2.287, Jenkins LTS prior to version 2.277.2.
solution: Update to version 2.287, 2.277.2 LTS or later.
insight: The following vulnerabilities exist:
- CVE-2021-21639: Jenkins does not validate the type of object created after loading the data
submitted to the cong.xml REST API endpoint of a node.
- CVE-2021-21640: Jenkins does not properly check that a newly created view has an allowed
name allowing attackers with View/Create permission to create views with invalid or used names.

Name: Jenkins < 2.319.2, < 2.330 CSRF Vulnerability - Linux
description: Jenkins is prone to a cross-site request forgery (CSRF) vulnerability.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
impact: This vulnerability allows attackers to trigger build of job without parameters.
solution: Update to version 2.330, 2.319.2 LTS or later.
insight: Jenkins version 2.329 and prior and 2.319.1 LTS and prior.
Jenkins does not require POST requests for the HTTP endpoint handling manual build requests
when no security realm is set, resulting in a CSRF vulnerability.

Name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
description: The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.
Quality of Detection (QoD): 80%
detection_result: The following response / ICMP packet has been received:
impact: This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.

Name: ICMP Timestamp Reply Information Disclosure
description: The remote host responded to an ICMP timestamp request.
detection_result: The following response / ICMP packet has been received:
detection_method: Sends an ICMP Timestamp (Type 13) request and checks if a Timestamp Reply (Type 14) is received.
impact: This information could theoretically be used to exploit weak time-based random number generators in other services.
solution: Various mitigations are possible:   - Disable the support for ICMP timestamp on the remote host completely  - Protect the remote host by a rewall, and block ICMP packets passing through the rewall in  either direction (either completely or only for untrusted networks)
insight: The Timestamp Reply is an ICMP message which replies to a Timestamp message. It consists of the originating timestamp sent by the sender of the Timestamp as well as a receive timestamp and a transmit timestamp.

Name: Eclipse Jetty Session Vulnerability (GHSA-m6cp-vxjx-65j6) - Linux
description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to a vulnerability in the session management.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Eclipse Jetty Session Vulnerability (GHSA-m6cp-vxjx-65j6) - Linux
product_detection_result: Method: Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP)
impact: If an exception is thrown from the SessionListener#sessionDestroyed() method, then the session ID is not invalidated in the session ID manager. On deployments with clustered sessions and multiple contexts this can result in a session not being invalidated. This can result in an application used on a shared computer being left logged in.
insight: If an exception is thrown from the SessionListener#sessionDestroyed() method, then the session ID is not invalidated in the session ID manager. On deployments with clustered sessions and multiple contexts this can result in a session not being invalidated. This can result in an application used on a shared computer being left logged in.

Name: Eclipse Jetty XXE Vulnerability (GHSA-58qw-p7qm-5rvh) - Linux
description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to a XML external entity (XXE) vulnerability in the XMLParser.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
Details: Eclipse Jetty XXE Vulnerability (GHSA-58qw-p7qm-5rvh) - Linux
product_detection_result: Method: Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP)
impact: XmlParser is being used when parsing Jetty's xml configuration files. An attacker might exploit this vulnerability in order to achieve SSRF or cause a denial of service.
One possible scenario is importing a (remote) malicious WAR into a Jetty's server, while the WAR includes a malicious web.xml.
insight: XmlParser is being used when parsing Jetty's xml configuration files. An attacker might exploit this vulnerability in order to achieve SSRF or cause a denial of service.
One possible scenario is importing a (remote) malicious WAR into a Jetty's server, while the WAR includes a malicious web.xml.

Name: Eclipse Jetty CgiServlet Vulnerability (GHSA-3gh6-v5v9-6v9j) - Linux
description: Eclipse Jetty is prone to a vulnerability in the CgiServlet.
detection_method: Checks if a vulnerable version is present on the target host.
product_detection_result: Detected by Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty Detection (HTTP) (OID: 1.3.6.1.4.1.25623.1.0.800953)
impact: Users of the CgiServlet with a very specific command structure may have the wrong command executed. If a user sends a request to a org.eclipse.jetty.servlets.CGI Servlet for a binary with a space in its name, the servlet will escape the command by wrapping it in quotation marks. This wrapped command, plus an optional command prefix, will then be executed through a call to Runtime.exec. If the original binary name provided by the user contains a quotation mark followed by a space, the resulting command line will contain multiple tokens instead of one.
insight: Users of the CgiServlet with a very specific command structure may have the wrong command executed. If a user sends a request to a org.eclipse.jetty.servlets.CGI Servlet for a binary with a space in its name, the servlet will escape the command by wrapping it in quotation marks. This wrapped command, plus an optional command prefix, will then be executed through a call to Runtime.exec. If the original binary name provided by the user contains a quotation mark followed by a space, the resulting command line will contain multiple tokens instead of one.

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between: Packet 1: 1114387995 Packet 2: 1114389107
impact: A side

Name: TCP timestamps
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps as defined in RFC 1323. A side effect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.
solution: To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.

description: HTTP based detection of Mort Bay / Eclipse Jetty.

Name: TCP Timestamps Information Disclosure
description: The remote host implements TCP timestamps and therefore allows to compute the uptime.
detection_result: It was detected that the host implements RFC1323/RFC7323.
The following timestamps were retrieved with a delay of 1 seconds in-between:
detection_method: Special IP packets are forged and sent with a little delay in between to the target IP. The responses are searched for a timestamps. If found, the timestamps are reported.
impact: A side eect of this feature is that the uptime of the remote host can sometimes be computed.
solution: To disable TCP timestamps on linux add the line 'net.ipv4.tcp_timestamps = 0' to /etc/sysctl.conf. Execute 'sysctl -p' to apply the settings at runtime.
To disable TCP timestamps on Windows execute 'netsh int tcp set global timestamps=disabled'
Starting with Windows Server 2008 and Vista, the timestamp can not be completely disabled.

The default behavior of the TCP/IP stack on this Systems is to not use the Timestamp options when initiating TCP connections, but use them if the TCP peer that is initiating communication includes them in their synchronize (SYN) segment.
See the references for more information.
insight: The remote host implements TCP timestamps, as dened by RFC1323/RFC7323.

Name: robot.txt / robots.txt exists on the Web Server (HTTP)
description: Web Servers can use a le called /robot(s).txt to ask search engines to ignore certain les and directories. By nature this le can not be used to protect private les from public read access.
detection_result: # we don't want robots to click "build" links
solution: Review the content of the /robot(s).txt le and consider removing the les from the server or protect them in other ways in case you actually intended non-public availability.
insight: Any serious web search engine will honor the /robot(s).txt le and not scan the les and directories listed there.
Any entries listed in this le are not even hidden anymore.
log_method: Details: robot.txt / robots.txt exists on the Web Server (HTTP)

Name: Traceroute
description: Collect information about the network route and network distance between the scanner host and the target host.
detection_result: Network route from scanner (172.30.0.2) to target (172.31.1.49):
Network distance between scanner and target: 2
insight: For internal networks, the distances are usually small, often less than 4 hosts between scanner and target. For public targets the distance is greater and might be 10 hosts or more.
log_method: A combination of the protocols ICMP and TCP is used to determine the route. This method is applicable for IPv4 only and it is also known as 'traceroute'.
